brief_justification: Paper A is a kinetic-transport calculation: it solves the linearized Boltzmann equation for a continuous electron distribution function, decomposing it into angular harmonics whose even/odd components relax at different rates to predict a magnetoresistance signature. Paper B is a methodological survey of social simulation paradigms (rule-based agents, LLM-driven agents, data-driven digital twins) with no governing equation, distribution function, or collision/relaxation dynamics. The only faint surface overlap is that both involve 'many interacting units', but the driving machinery (a continuum kinetic PDE vs. discrete behavioral-rule/AI agent modeling) is entirely different, so no role-for-role mapping holds.
